text:
As optical sensor quality continues to evolve, the demand for processing high-resolution visual data has grown exponentially. Specifically, the prevalence of 4K and 8K ultra-high-definition (UHD) content poses significant challenges for traditional image processing pipelines. In this paper, we address the problem of low-light image enhancement (LLIE) in the context of UHD resolution. We first introduce UHD-LOL, a large-scale dataset featuring 4K and 8K image pairs, and use it to conduct a systematic benchmarking study of current LLIE algorithms. 

To better handle the computational demands of high-resolution processing, we propose LLFormer, a novel transformer-based architecture designed for efficient enhancement. The primary innovations of LLFormer include an axis-based multi-head self-attention mechanism and a cross-layer attention fusion block, which together reduce computational complexity to a linear scale without sacrificing the ability to capture long-range dependencies. Extensive experiments on our UHD-LOL dataset, as well as the standard LOL and MIT-Adobe FiveK benchmarks, demonstrate that LLFormer achieves state-of-the-art performance. Furthermore, we show that using LLFormer as a pre-processing step significantly boosts the accuracy of downstream tasks, such as face detection, in poorly lit environments. Our source code and pre-trained models are available at https://github.com/TaoWangzj/LLFormer.
